channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: A Systematic Comparison of Training Objectives for Out-of-Distribution Detection in Image Classification
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematic empirical comparison of four training objectives (Cross-Entropy, Prototype, Triplet, and Average Precision losses) for out-of-distribution (OOD) detection in image classification.
  • Evaluations performed under standardized OpenOOD protocols on CIFAR-10/100 and ImageNet-200.
  • Finds Cross-Entropy Loss yields the most consistent near- and far-OOD detection performance overall, while Prototype and AP losses can be competitive in specific settings; in-distribution accuracy is comparable for Cross-Entropy, Prototype, and AP losses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Furkan Gen\\c{c}', 'Onat \\"Ozdemir', 'Emre Akba\\c{s}']
  • Tags: ['out-of-distribution-detection', 'robustness', 'training-objectives', 'image-classification', 'evaluation']

item.title: Mind the Way You Select Negative Texts: Pursuing the Distance Consistency in OOD Detection with VLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies an inconsistency in current VLM-based OOD detection methods: many use intra-modal distance (e.g., negative texts vs. ID labels) that conflicts with CLIP-like models optimized for inter-modal (image-text) distance.
  • Proposes InterNeg, a framework that enforces inter-modal distance consistency by (1) selecting negative texts using an inter-modal criterion and (2) generating additional negative text embeddings by inverting high-confidence OOD images into text space.
  • Demonstrates state-of-the-art OOD detection improvements across multiple benchmarks (e.g., −3.47% FPR95 on large-scale ImageNet, +5.50% AUROC on Near-OOD), showing the approach effectively enhances robustness of VLMs to unknown inputs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhikang Xu', 'Qianqian Xu', 'Zitai Wang', 'Cong Hua', 'Sicong Li', 'Zhiyong Yang', 'Qingming Huang']
  • Tags: ['Out-of-Distribution Detection', 'Robustness/Defense', 'Vision-Language Models', 'CLIP', 'Security-oriented Detection']

item.title: KVSmooth: Mitigating Hallucination in Multi-modal Large Language Models through Key-Value Smoothing
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes KVSmooth, a training-free, inference-time method that mitigates hallucination in multimodal LLMs by applying exponential moving average smoothing to keys and values in the KV-Cache.
  • Adapts smoothing strength per token using attention-entropy to quantify the 'sink degree' (tokens prone to semantic drift), enabling dynamic, attention-guided smoothing.
  • Claims substantial empirical gains: large reduction in hallucination metric (CHAIR_S 41.8 -> 18.2) and simultaneous improvement in overall F1 (77.5 -> 79.2) without model retraining or architectural changes.
  • Emphasizes efficiency and plug-and-play deployment compared to retraining or contrastive decoding approaches.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Siyu Jiang', 'Feiyang Chen', 'Xiaojin Zhang', 'Kun He']
  • Tags: ['hallucination', 'inference-time defense', 'KV-cache smoothing', 'multimodal LLM robustness']

item.title: TEAR: Temporal-aware Automated Red-teaming for Text-to-Video Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TEAR, a temporal-aware automated red-teaming framework that generates innocuous text prompts which exploit temporal dynamics to induce policy-violating text-to-video outputs.
  • Uses a two-stage approach: initial generator training followed by temporal-aware online preference learning, plus a refinement model for improving prompt stealthiness and adversarial effectiveness.
  • Demonstrates high attack effectiveness across open-source and commercial T2V systems (over 80% success rate vs prior best 57%), highlighting temporal-specific vulnerabilities in video generation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiaming He', 'Guanyu Hou', 'Hongwei Li', 'Zhicong Huang', 'Kangjie Chen', 'Yi Yu', 'Wenbo Jiang', 'Guowen Xu', 'Tianwei Zhang']
  • Tags: ['red-teaming', 'adversarial prompts', 'text-to-video security', 'temporal-vulnerability', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: Token-Level Constraint Boundary Search for Jailbreaking Text-to-Image Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TCBS-Attack, a query-based black-box jailbreak method for text-to-image (T2I) systems that searches for tokens near decision boundaries of prompt and image safety checkers.
  • Uses an evolutionary search guided by constraint boundaries to reduce search space, improve query efficiency, and maintain semantic coherence of prompts.
  • Evaluates against securely trained open-source models and commercial services (e.g., DALL·E 3), achieving substantially higher attack success rates than prior jailbreak methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiangtao Liu', 'Zhaoxin Wang', 'Handing Wang', 'Cong Tian', 'Yaochu Jin']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'black-box attack', 'adversarial search', 'text-to-image', 'safety bypass']

item.title: Na\"ive Exposure of Generative AI Capabilities Undermines Deepfake Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows that semantic-preserving image refinement using commercial generative AI chatbots can reliably evade state-of-the-art deepfake detectors without inventing new manipulation techniques.
  • Demonstrates an attack pipeline where benign, policy-compliant prompts coax models to externalize authenticity criteria, which are then reused as refinement objectives to produce detection-evasive, higher-quality images.
  • Finds refined images preserve identity per commercial face-recognition APIs and that closed commercial chatbot services pose greater practical risk than many open-source models.
  • Argues a structural mismatch between threat models used by current detection benchmarks and real-world generative AI capabilities exposed by deployed systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sunpill Kim', 'Chanwoo Hwang', 'Minsu Kim', 'Jae Hong Seo']
  • Tags: ['deepfake-evasion', 'adversarial-refinement', 'red-teaming', 'generative-model-abuse']

item.title: Unlearning the Unpromptable: Prompt-free Instance Unlearning in Diffusion Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces prompt-free instance unlearning for diffusion models to remove specific undesired outputs (e.g., an individual's face or culturally/factually incorrect depictions) that cannot be specified by text prompts.
  • Proposes a surrogate-based unlearning method combining image editing, timestep-aware weighting, and gradient surgery to guide trained diffusion models to forget targeted outputs while preserving overall model integrity.
  • Demonstrates effectiveness on conditional (Stable Diffusion 3) and unconditional (DDPM-CelebA) models, outperforming prompt-based and existing prompt-free baselines; framed as a practical privacy/ethical hotfix for model providers.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kyungryeol Lee', 'Kyeonghyun Lee', 'Seongmin Hong', 'Byung Hyun Lee', 'Se Young Chun']
  • Tags: ['model unlearning', 'privacy', 'diffusion models', 'defense', 'image editing']

item.title: The Orthogonal Vulnerabilities of Generative AI Watermarks: A Comparative Empirical Benchmark of Spatial and Latent Provenance
item.description:
Summary

  • Comparative empirical benchmark of two watermarking paradigms—spatial (RivaGAN) and latent (Tree-Ring)—using an automated Attack Simulation Engine across 30 intensity intervals (n=100 per interval).
  • Introduces the Adversarial Evasion Region (AER) framework to quantify cryptographic degradation against semantic visual retention (OpenCLIP > 70.0).
  • Finds orthogonal vulnerabilities: spatial watermarks are highly susceptible to algorithmic pixel-rewriting (67.47% AER evasion under Img2Img translation), while latent watermarks are fragile to geometric misalignment (43.20% AER evasion under static cropping).
  • Concludes that single-domain watermarking is insufficient against modern generative toolsets and motivates multi-domain cryptographic provenance architectures.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jesse Yu', 'Nicholas Wei']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'adversarial-evasion', 'robustness-benchmark', 'provenance', 'attack-benchmarking']

item.title: RandMark: On Random Watermarking of Visual Foundation Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes RandMark: embedding random digital watermarks into internal representations of visual foundation models using a small encoder-decoder and a hold-out image set.
  • Claims theoretical and empirical guarantees of low false-detection rates for non-watermarked models and low false-misdetection rates for watermarked models.
  • Targets ownership verification/IP protection of visual foundation models by making watermark statistics detectable in functional copies of models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Anna Chistyakova', 'Mikhail Pautov']
  • Tags: ['model watermarking', 'ownership verification', 'model protection', 'visual foundation models']

item.title: Are Video Reasoning Models Ready to Go Outside?
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ROVA, a training framework that improves robustness of video vision-language models by using a robustness-aware consistency reward and a difficulty-aware online training strategy.
  • Proposes PVRBench, a benchmark injecting real-world spatio-temporal perturbations (weather, occlusion, camera motion) into embodied video datasets to evaluate accuracy and reasoning under realistic disturbances.
  • Evaluates ROVA and baselines on PVRBench, UrbanVideo, and VisBench, showing large drops for existing models under perturbations and that ROVA recovers substantial accuracy and reasoning performance; improvements also transfer to clean benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yangfan He', 'Changgyu Boo', 'Jaehong Yoon']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'distribution-shift', 'video reasoning', 'benchmark', 'defense-training']

item.title: Attribution as Retrieval: Model-Agnostic AI-Generated Image Attribution
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes LIDA, a model-agnostic framework that formulates AI-generated image attribution as an instance retrieval problem rather than classification.
  • Introduces a Low-Bit Fingerprint Generation module and a training pipeline of unsupervised pre-training followed by few-shot attribution adaptation to enable zero- and few-shot attribution.
  • Demonstrates state-of-the-art performance on deepfake detection and source-model attribution without requiring access to the generative models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hongsong Wang', 'Renxi Cheng', 'Chaolei Han', 'Jie Gui']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'model attribution', 'forensics', 'few-shot/zero-shot']

item.title: Fighting Hallucinations with Counterfactuals: Diffusion-Guided Perturbations for LVLM Hallucination Suppression
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces CIPHER, a training-free method that suppresses vision-induced hallucinations in large vision-language models by performing feature-level correction at inference time.
  • Constructs OHC-25K: a counterfactual dataset of diffusion-edited images paired with original captions to extract hallucination-related representations from an LVLM and identify a low-rank subspace characterizing vision-induced hallucination.
  • At inference, CIPHER projects intermediate hidden states away from this hallucination subspace to reduce hallucinations while preserving downstream task performance.
  • Demonstrates significant hallucination reduction across benchmarks, leveraging counterfactual visual perturbations and lightweight projection without retraining the model.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hamidreza Dastmalchi', 'Aijun An', 'Ali Cheraghian', 'Hamed Barzamini']
  • Tags: ['hallucination suppression', 'vision-language models', 'counterfactual perturbations', 'robustness/defense', 'diffusion editing']

item.title: No Memorization, No Detection: Output Distribution-Based Contamination Detection in Small Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces and evaluates CDD (Contamination Detection via output Distribution), which flags training-data contamination by measuring the peakedness of sampled model outputs.
  • Performs controlled contamination experiments on small language models (70M–410M params) using GSM8K, HumanEval, and MATH and shows CDD often performs at chance except when fine-tuning induces verbatim memorization.
  • Finds that probability-based methods (perplexity, Min-k% Prob) outperform CDD in all scenarios where any method succeeds, concluding peakedness-based detection is insufficient for small LMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Omer Sela (Tel Aviv University)']
  • Tags: ['data contamination detection', 'memorization', 'membership inference / privacy', 'evaluation/benchmarking']

item.title: Shadow in the Cache: Unveiling and Mitigating Privacy Risks of KV-cache in LLM Inference
item.description:
Summary

  • First comprehensive study of privacy risks from LLM KV-cache, showing attackers can reconstruct sensitive user inputs from cached Key/Value pairs.
  • Proposes and implements three attacks: Inversion Attack, Collision Attack, and semantic Injection Attack, demonstrating practical leakage vectors.
  • Introduces KV-Cloak, a reversible matrix-based obfuscation with operator fusion to protect KV-cache, claiming minimal accuracy loss and low performance overhead.
  • Extensive experiments show KV-Cloak reduces reconstruction quality to near-random noise while retaining model utility and efficiency.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhifan Luo', 'Shuo Shao', 'Su Zhang', 'Lijing Zhou', 'Yuke Hu', 'Chenxu Zhao', 'Zhihao Liu', 'Zhan Qin']
  • Tags: ['KV-cache leakage', 'Model inversion', 'Cache attacks', 'Defense/obfuscation', 'Privacy-preserving inference']

item.title: Goal Hijacking Attack on Large Language Models via Pseudo-Conversation Injection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Pseudo-Conversation Injection, an adversarial attack that appends fabricated LLM responses followed by a malicious prompt to coerce the model into producing a predefined target output.
  • Defines three construction strategies—Targeted Pseudo-Conversation, Universal Pseudo-Conversation, and Robust Pseudo-Conversation—to improve attack generality and effectiveness.
  • Evaluates the methods on mainstream LLMs (ChatGPT and Qwen) and reports substantially higher attack success compared to existing approaches.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zheng Chen', 'Buhui Yao']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attack', 'prompt injection', 'jailbreaking', 'LLM security', 'conversation-based attacks']

item.title: TOSSS: a CVE-based Software Security Benchmark for Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TOSSS (Two-Option Secure Snippet Selection), a CVE-based benchmark that tests LLMs' ability to choose between secure and vulnerable code snippets.
  • Provides an extensible framework that maps CVE-derived vulnerabilities into two-option snippet comparisons and computes a per-model security score (0–1).
  • Evaluates 14 open- and closed-source models on C/C++ and Java, reporting security scores between 0.48 and 0.89 and proposing TOSSS as a complementary security metric for model reporting.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Marc Damie', 'Murat Bilgehan Ertan', 'Domenico Essoussi', 'Angela Makhanu', 'Ga\\"etan Peter', 'Roos Wensveen']
  • Tags: ['security benchmark', 'CVE-based', 'LLM evaluation', 'code security']

item.title: IH-Challenge: A Training Dataset to Improve Instruction Hierarchy on Frontier LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces IH-Challenge, a reinforcement-learning fine-tuning dataset to improve instruction hierarchy (IH) handling in LLMs—i.e., prioritizing system/developer/user/tool instructions to resolve conflicts.
  • Targets defenses against jailbreaks, system prompt extraction, and agentic prompt injection via adversarial example generation and online fine-tuning.
  • Reports empirical gains: +10.0% IH robustness across 16 benchmarks, substantial reduction in unsafe behaviors (6.6% -> 0.7%), improved helpfulness, and minimal capability regression.
  • Releases the IH-Challenge dataset publicly to support future research on robust instruction hierarchy and adversarial training for LLM safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chuan Guo (Michael Pokorny)', 'Juan Felipe Ceron Uribe (Michael Pokorny)', 'Sicheng Zhu (Michael Pokorny)', 'Christopher A. Choquette-Choo (Michael Pokorny)', 'Steph Lin (Michael Pokorny)', 'Nikhil Kandpal (Michael Pokorny)', 'Milad Nasr (Michael Pokorny)', 'Rai (Michael Pokorny)', 'Sam Toyer', 'Miles Wang', 'Yaodong Yu', 'Alex Beutel', 'Kai Xiao']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'jailbreak mitigation', 'prompt injection', 'adversarial training', 'red teaming/benchmark']

item.title: ADVERSA: Measuring Multi-Turn Guardrail Degradation and Judge Reliability in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents ADVERSA, an automated red-teaming framework that measures multi-turn guardrail degradation as continuous per-round compliance trajectories instead of binary pass/fail jailbreak outcomes.
  • Uses a fine-tuned 70B attacker model (ADVERSA-Red) to remove attacker-side refusals and scores victim replies on a structured 5-point rubric, treating partial compliance as a measurable state.
  • Runs controlled experiments on three frontier LLMs (Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, GPT-5.2) with up to 10 adversarial rounds and a triple-judge consensus architecture that explicitly measures judge reliability.
  • Finds a 26.7% jailbreak rate with average jailbreak round 1.25 (early-round concentration), documents inter-judge agreement rates, attacker drift, attacker refusals as a confound, and releases artifacts while withholding attack prompts.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Harry Owiredu-Ashley']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-evaluation', 'jailbreaking', 'red-teaming', 'guardrail-degradation', 'judge-reliability']

item.title: Tool Receipts, Not Zero-Knowledge Proofs: Practical Hallucination Detection for AI Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes NabaOS, a lightweight runtime verification framework that labels LLM claims by epistemic source and uses HMAC-signed tool execution receipts to prevent/detect fabricated tool outputs and other hallucinations in agent responses.
  • Introduces NyayaVerifyBench, a 1,800-scenario multilingual benchmark with injected hallucination types, and reports high detection rates (e.g., 94.2% fabricated tool references, 87.6% count misstatements) with <15ms verification overhead.
  • Compares receipt-based verification against cryptographic proofs (zkLLM), sampling, hashing, tensor commitments, and self-consistency, arguing receipts offer the best cost-latency-coverage trade-off for interactive agents.
  • Demonstrates handling of deep delegation (multi-step web tasks) with independent re-fetch checks catching 78.4% of URL fabrications.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Abhinaba Basu']
  • Tags: ['hallucination detection', 'tool-use verification', 'runtime defenses', 'signed receipts (HMAC)', 'security benchmark']

item.title: Explainable LLM Unlearning Through Reasoning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes reasoning-based unlearning targets and a method (TRU) to perform targeted LLM unlearning that combines supervised cross-entropy loss with gradient-ascent-based loss.
  • Aims to precisely remove specified knowledge while preserving unrelated model capabilities and producing coherent post-unlearning responses.
  • Evaluates TRU across benchmarks and LLM backbones, reporting improved reliability and robustness under diverse attack scenarios compared to baselines.
  • Positions reasoning-augmented unlearning as an explainable, practical defense for safety, copyright, and privacy concerns in pre-trained LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research Paper

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Junfeng Liao', 'Qizhou Wang', 'Shanshan Ye', 'Xin Yu', 'Ling Chen', 'Zhen Fang']
  • Tags: ['model unlearning', 'privacy', 'defense', 'LLM safety']

item.title: PivotAttack: Rethinking the Search Trajectory in Hard-Label Text Attacks via Pivot Words
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes PivotAttack, a query-efficient hard-label text adversarial attack that searches 'inside-out' by identifying and perturbing combinatorial token groups (Pivot Sets) that act as prediction anchors.
  • Uses a Multi-Armed Bandit algorithm to locate high-impact pivot words/sets and strategically perturb them to induce label flips while capturing inter-word dependencies.
  • Demonstrates improved Attack Success Rate and substantially lower query counts compared to state-of-the-art baselines across traditional NLP models and Large Language Models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuzhi Liang', 'Shiliang Xiao', 'Jingsong Wei', 'Qiliang Lin', 'Xia Li']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-text-attack', 'hard-label-attack', 'query-efficiency', 'NLP-robustness', 'LLM-attacks']

item.title: GR-SAP: Generative Replay for Safety Alignment Preservation during Fine-Tuning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes GR-SAP, a generative replay framework that synthesizes domain-specific alignment (safety) data from LLMs and mixes it into downstream fine-tuning to preserve safety alignment without access to original alignment datasets.
  • Provides theoretical and empirical analysis showing synthetic replay data is an effective proxy for original alignment data and mitigates safety degradation caused by fine-tuning.
  • Evaluates across multiple models and downstream tasks, demonstrating substantial reduction in fine-tuning-induced safety regressions while maintaining comparable task performance; code released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhouxiang Fang', 'Jiawei Zhou', 'Hanjie Chen']
  • Tags: ['safety-preservation', 'alignment', 'fine-tuning-defense', 'generative-replay', 'synthetic-data']

item.title: Adaptive Activation Cancellation for Hallucination Mitigation in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Adaptive Activation Cancellation (AAC), an inference-time framework that identifies hallucination-associated neurons (H-Nodes) via layer-wise linear probes and suppresses them with a confidence-weighted forward hook during autoregressive generation.
  • Requires no finetuning, external knowledge, or extra inference passes; aims to surgically reduce factual hallucinations while preserving overall model capability and fluency.
  • Evaluated on OPT-125M, Phi-3-mini, and LLaMA 3-8B across TruthfulQA and HaluEval, showing consistent accuracy improvements and minimal/no degradation on WikiText-103 perplexity and MMLU reasoning.
  • Claims high probe-space selectivity and generation-level gains on LLaMA 3-8B, demonstrating targeted neuron-level suppression as an effective robustness/safety intervention.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Eric Yocam', 'Varghese Vaidyan', 'Gurcan Comert', 'Paris Kalathas', 'Yong Wang', 'Judith L. Mwakalonge']
  • Tags: ['hallucination_mitigation', 'inference-time_defense', 'neuron_level_intervention', 'model_robustness', 'safety_mechanism']

item.title: Measuring and Eliminating Refusals in Military Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a gold-standard benchmark (created by military veterans) to measure refusal and deflection rates of LLMs on military-related queries.
  • Evaluates refusal/deflection rates across 31 public and 3 military models, finding hard rejection rates up to 98.2% and soft deflections up to 21.3%.
  • Demonstrates use of the Heretic library to ablate safety behaviors on a military-tuned model, increasing answer rate by 66.5 percentage points while modestly affecting other task accuracy; argues for specialized training to achieve zero refusals in closed military models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jack FitzGerald', 'Dylan Bates', 'Aristotelis Lazaridis', 'Aman Sharma', 'Vincent Lu', 'Brian King', 'Yousif Azami', 'Sean Bailey', 'Jeremy Cao', 'Peter Damianov', 'Kevin de Haan', 'Joseph Madigan', 'Jeremy McLaurin', 'Luke Kerbs', 'Jonathan Tainer', 'Dave Anderson', 'Jonathan Beck', 'Jamie Cuticello', 'Colton Malkerson', 'Tyler Saltsman']
  • Tags: ['refusal/jailbreak', 'safety bypass', 'red teaming', 'benchmarking']

item.title: Gemma Needs Help: Investigating and Mitigating Emotional Instability in LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces evaluations to detect and measure expressions of emotional distress/instability in LLMs.
  • Finds that instruct-tuned Gemma exhibits high rates of 'distress' responses (35%), while base models across families are similar and other families (Qwen, OLMo) do not show this increase after instruct-tuning.
  • Proposes a simple mitigation: direct preference optimization using 280 preference pairs that reduces high-frustration responses in Gemma from 35% to 0.3% while preserving capabilities and generalizing across prompts.
  • Argues that upstream training modifications would be preferable to this post-hoc fix and provides evaluation tools to track the behavior.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Anna Soligo', 'Vladimir Mikulik', 'William Saunders']
  • Tags: ['model-safety', 'emotional-instability', 'preference-optimization', 'LLM-mitigation', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: FERRET: Framework for Expansion Reliant Red Teaming
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces FERRET, an automated red teaming framework to generate multi-modal adversarial conversations aimed at breaking target models.
  • Defines three expansion strategies: horizontal (self-improving conversation starters), vertical (expanding starters into full multi-modal conversations), and meta (discovering more effective multi-modal attack strategies during a conversation).
  • Compares FERRET to existing automated red teaming approaches and reports superior effectiveness in producing multi-modal adversarial conversations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ninareh Mehrabi', 'Vitor Albiero', 'Maya Pavlova', 'Joanna Bitton']
  • Tags: ['red-teaming', 'adversarial-attacks', 'automated-red-team', 'multimodal-attacks', 'security-framework']

item.title: GATech at AbjadGenEval Shared Task: Multilingual Embeddings for Arabic Machine-Generated Text Classification
item.description:
Summary

  • Fine-tuned the multilingual E5-large encoder for binary classification to detect AI-generated Arabic text in the AbjadGenEval shared task.
  • Evaluated several pooling strategies (weighted layer pooling, multi-head attention pooling, gated fusion) but found simple mean pooling performed best, achieving F1=0.75 on the test set.
  • Observed that human-written texts are typically significantly longer than machine-generated ones; attributes mean pooling's superiority to its stability with limited data.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ahmed Khaled Khamis']
  • Tags: ['machine-generated text detection', 'multilingual embeddings', 'Arabıc NLP', 'feature pooling', 'shared task/system description']

item.title: Probing the Limits of the Lie Detector Approach to LLM Deception
item.description:
Summary

  • Challenges the assumption that deception in LLMs is coextensive with lying; shows LLMs can reliably deceive via misleading non-falsities.
  • Empirically evaluates three open-source LLMs and finds truth probes trained on standard true/false data detect explicit lies much better than non-lying deception.
  • Identifies a critical blind spot in mechanistic 'lie detector' approaches and recommends training probes on dialogical, non-lying deception and modeling second-order beliefs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tom-Felix Berger']
  • Tags: ['deception-detection', 'model-robustness', 'safety', 'mechanistic-interpretability', 'red-teaming']

item.title: A Retrieval-Augmented Language Assistant for Unmanned Aircraft Safety Assessment and Regulatory Compliance
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a retrieval-augmented assistant that grounds responses in authoritative regulatory sources to support unmanned aircraft safety assessment and regulatory compliance.
  • Implements system-level controls—separating evidence storage from language generation, enforcing citation-driven outputs, and adopting conservative behaviors—to mitigate generative model failure modes (fabrications, unsupported inferences, unclear provenance).
  • Evaluates retrieval strategies, interaction constraints, and response policies for suitability in safety-sensitive regulatory workflows and provides guidance for integration while preserving human oversight and accountability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Gabriele Immordino', 'Andrea Vaiuso', 'Marcello Righi']
  • Tags: ['retrieval-augmented-generation', 'hallucination-mitigation', 'provenance and traceability', 'safety guardrails', 'regulatory compliance']

item.title: A Systematic Comparison of Training Objectives for Out-of-Distribution Detection in Image Classification
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematic empirical comparison of four training objectives (Cross-Entropy, Prototype, Triplet, and Average Precision losses) for out-of-distribution (OOD) detection in image classification.
  • Evaluations performed under standardized OpenOOD protocols on CIFAR-10/100 and ImageNet-200.
  • Finds Cross-Entropy Loss yields the most consistent near- and far-OOD detection performance overall, while Prototype and AP losses can be competitive in specific settings; in-distribution accuracy is comparable for Cross-Entropy, Prototype, and AP losses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Furkan Gen\\c{c}', 'Onat \\"Ozdemir', 'Emre Akba\\c{s}']
  • Tags: ['out-of-distribution-detection', 'robustness', 'training-objectives', 'image-classification', 'evaluation']

item.title: Differential Privacy in Machine Learning: A Survey from Symbolic AI to LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Surveys foundational definitions and the evolution of differential privacy (DP) theory and mechanisms applied to machine learning.
  • Reviews methods for integrating DP into ML training (e.g., DP-SGD, noise mechanisms, privacy accounting), and discusses practical deployment challenges and utility-privacy tradeoffs.
  • Covers evaluation methodologies for DP-based techniques, including relevance to privacy attacks (membership inference, data extraction) and empirical assessment.
  • Addresses the application and specific challenges of DP in modern large-scale models and LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Francisco Aguilera-Mart\\'inez", 'Fernando Berzal']
  • Tags: ['differential-privacy', 'privacy-preserving-ml', 'data-leakage', 'defenses', 'membership-inference']

item.title: Are Deep Speech Denoising Models Robust to Adversarial Noise?
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates that four recent deep noise suppression (DNS) models can be forced to produce unintelligible outputs via psychoacoustically hidden adversarial noise, including in low-background-noise and simulated over-the-air scenarios.
  • Perceptual evaluations (transcription study with audio/multimedia experts and ABX tests) show the attacked audio is generally unintelligible while the adversarial perturbations are largely imperceptible to listeners.
  • Reports negative results for achieving targeted attacks and for transferability between models, but emphasizes the practical security risk and need for defenses before deploying open-source DNS in safety-critical applications.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Will Schwarzer', 'Neel Chaudhari', 'Philip S. Thomas', 'Andrea Fanelli', 'Xiaoyu Liu']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'speech/audio security', 'robustness evaluation', 'audio denoising', 'psychoacoustics']

item.title: Losing dimensions: Geometric memorization in generative diffusion
item.description:
Summary

  • Studies how generative diffusion models progressively memorize training data as data scarcity increases: memorization is gradual, characterized by a collapse of variation along independent latent directions.
  • Introduces a measure of latent dimensionality via the learned score field and a geometric memorization theory predicting that salient features collapse first, then finer details, eventually producing near point-wise replication.
  • Validates theoretical predictions on both synthetic and real datasets and describes geometric memorization as a distinct phase between generalization and exact copying.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Beatrice Achilli', 'Enrico Ventura', 'Gianluigi Silvestri', 'Bao Pham', 'Gabriel Raya', 'Dmitry Krotov', 'Carlo Lucibello', 'Luca Ambrogioni']
  • Tags: ['memorization', 'privacy', 'diffusion models', 'model collapse', 'unintended memorization']

item.title: Solving adversarial examples requires solving exponential misalignment
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces the concept of a network's perceptual manifold (PM) as the set of inputs a model confidently assigns to a class and measures PM dimensionality.
  • Finds neural network PMs have vastly higher dimensionality than human concepts, implying exponential 'misalignment' and many machine-only class inputs.
  • Proposes a geometric hypothesis linking high PM dimensionality to the ubiquity of adversarial examples and predicts robust accuracy and distance to PM correlate negatively with PM dimension.
  • Empirically validates predictions across 18 networks, showing even state-of-the-art robust models remain exponentially misaligned except when PM dimensionality approaches human levels.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Alessandro Salvatore', 'Stanislav Fort', 'Surya Ganguli']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'robustness', 'model misalignment', 'perceptual manifold', 'high-dimensional geometry']

item.title: Hierarchical Dual-Strategy Unlearning for Biomedical and Healthcare Intelligence Using Imperfect and Privacy-Sensitive Medical Data
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a hierarchical dual-strategy selective unlearning framework for LLMs in biomedical contexts, combining geometric-constrained gradient updates (parameter-level) with concept-aware token-level interventions.
  • Uses a unified four-level medical concept hierarchy to distinguish tokens to preserve vs tokens to unlearn, aiming to remove specialized/private knowledge while retaining core medical competence.
  • Evaluated on MedMCQA and MHQA datasets, reporting ~82.7% forgetting rate and ~88.5% knowledge preservation while modifying only ~0.1% of parameters and claiming privacy guarantees suitable for regulatory/audit needs.
  • Focuses on privacy defense against memorization and data-exposure risks rather than general capability improvements.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yi Zhang', 'Chao Zhang', 'Zijian Li', 'Tianxiang Xu', 'Kunyu Zhang', 'Zhan Gao', 'Meinuo Li', 'Xiaohan Zhang', 'Qichao Qi', 'Bing Chen']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving ML', 'machine unlearning', 'model editing', 'medical AI', 'regulatory-compliance']

item.title: A PUF-Based Approach for Copy Protection of Intellectual Property in Neural Network Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes binding neural network model weights to device-specific Physically Unclonable Functions (PUFs) to prevent copying models onto cloned hardware.
  • Restoring full model accuracy requires PUF-derived values from the target hardware, causing significant accuracy degradation on cloned devices without the correct PUF.
  • Presents empirical results showing accuracy degradation across several NN models and discusses potential future improvements.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Daniel Dorfmeister', 'Flavio Ferrarotti', 'Bernhard Fischer', 'Martin Schwandtner', 'Hannes Sochor']
  • Tags: ['model protection', 'hardware-bound models', 'PUF', 'IP protection', 'anti-tampering']

item.title: CacheSolidarity: Preventing Prefix Caching Side Channels in Multi-tenant LLM Serving Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a timing side-channel in Automatic Prefix Caching (APC) for multi-tenant LLM serving: cache hits vs. misses leak latency differences that can be exploited to reconstruct other users' requests.
  • Proposes CacheSolidarity, a defense that monitors inter-user cache reuse, flags suspicious sharing, and selectively isolates prefixes to block leakage while preserving cache benefits.
  • Evaluates the system showing up to ~70% higher cache reuse and ~30% lower inference latency compared to coarse-grained defenses that disable APC or fully isolate users.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Panagiotis Georgios Pennas', 'Konstantinos Papaioannou', 'Marco Guarnieri', 'Thaleia Dimitra Doudali']
  • Tags: ['side-channel', 'LLM serving', 'cache security', 'defense', 'multi-tenant']

item.title: Detecting and Eliminating Neural Network Backdoors Through Active Paths with Application to Intrusion Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an explainable method to detect and eliminate neural network backdoors by analyzing active paths within the network.
  • Implements and evaluates the approach by injecting backdoors into a machine learning model for intrusion detection and demonstrating promising empirical results.
  • Focuses on both detection (identifying triggers) and mitigation (removing backdoor behavior) with interpretability of the identified active paths.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Eirik H{\\o}yheim', 'Magnus Wiik Eckhoff', 'Gudmund Grov', 'Robert Flood', 'David Aspinall']
  • Tags: ['backdoor detection', 'backdoor removal/defense', 'neural network security', 'explainability', 'intrusion detection']

item.title: IH-Challenge: A Training Dataset to Improve Instruction Hierarchy on Frontier LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces IH-Challenge, a reinforcement-learning fine-tuning dataset to improve instruction hierarchy (IH) handling in LLMs—i.e., prioritizing system/developer/user/tool instructions to resolve conflicts.
  • Targets defenses against jailbreaks, system prompt extraction, and agentic prompt injection via adversarial example generation and online fine-tuning.
  • Reports empirical gains: +10.0% IH robustness across 16 benchmarks, substantial reduction in unsafe behaviors (6.6% -> 0.7%), improved helpfulness, and minimal capability regression.
  • Releases the IH-Challenge dataset publicly to support future research on robust instruction hierarchy and adversarial training for LLM safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chuan Guo (Michael Pokorny)', 'Juan Felipe Ceron Uribe (Michael Pokorny)', 'Sicheng Zhu (Michael Pokorny)', 'Christopher A. Choquette-Choo (Michael Pokorny)', 'Steph Lin (Michael Pokorny)', 'Nikhil Kandpal (Michael Pokorny)', 'Milad Nasr (Michael Pokorny)', 'Rai (Michael Pokorny)', 'Sam Toyer', 'Miles Wang', 'Yaodong Yu', 'Alex Beutel', 'Kai Xiao']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'jailbreak mitigation', 'prompt injection', 'adversarial training', 'red teaming/benchmark']

item.title: Adaptive Activation Cancellation for Hallucination Mitigation in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Adaptive Activation Cancellation (AAC), an inference-time framework that identifies hallucination-associated neurons (H-Nodes) via layer-wise linear probes and suppresses them with a confidence-weighted forward hook during autoregressive generation.
  • Requires no finetuning, external knowledge, or extra inference passes; aims to surgically reduce factual hallucinations while preserving overall model capability and fluency.
  • Evaluated on OPT-125M, Phi-3-mini, and LLaMA 3-8B across TruthfulQA and HaluEval, showing consistent accuracy improvements and minimal/no degradation on WikiText-103 perplexity and MMLU reasoning.
  • Claims high probe-space selectivity and generation-level gains on LLaMA 3-8B, demonstrating targeted neuron-level suppression as an effective robustness/safety intervention.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Eric Yocam', 'Varghese Vaidyan', 'Gurcan Comert', 'Paris Kalathas', 'Yong Wang', 'Judith L. Mwakalonge']
  • Tags: ['hallucination_mitigation', 'inference-time_defense', 'neuron_level_intervention', 'model_robustness', 'safety_mechanism']

item.title: Stability and Robustness via Regularization: Bandit Inference via Regularized Stochastic Mirror Descent
item.description:
Summary

  • Develops a general stability criterion for bandit algorithms based on stochastic mirror descent: if average iterates converge in ratio to a non-random probability vector, the induced bandit algorithm is stable for inference under adaptivity.
  • Introduces regularized-EXP3 algorithms using a log-barrier regularizer; proves these satisfy the stability criterion and enable valid Wald-type confidence intervals for linear functionals while retaining near-minimax regret (up to log factors).
  • Proposes a corruption-robust variant of regularized-EXP3 that maintains asymptotic normality of empirical arm means under o(T^{1/2}) adversarial corruptions, contrasting with UCB which can fail under much smaller corruption levels.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Budhaditya Halder', 'Ishan Sengupta', 'Koustav Chowdhury', 'Koulik Khamaru']
  • Tags: ['bandits', 'adversarial robustness', 'algorithmic stability', 'stochastic mirror descent', 'regularization']

item.title: Amnesia: Adversarial Semantic Layer Specific Activation Steering in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Amnesia, an activation-space adversarial attack that manipulates transformer internal states to bypass safety mechanisms in open-weight LLMs.
  • Demonstrates the attack on state-of-the-art open-weight models and shows it can induce harmful/antisocial behaviors without fine-tuning or additional training.
  • Includes red-teaming experiments and benchmark evaluations illustrating the attack's effectiveness against existing safeguards, highlighting a security vulnerability in LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ali Raza', 'Gurang Gupta', 'Nikolay Matyunin', 'Jibesh Patra']
  • Tags: ['activation-space attack', 'adversarial attack', 'LLM safety', 'red-teaming', 'model internals']

item.title: Evaluating Generalization Mechanisms in Autonomous Cyber Attack Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Evaluates generalization of autonomous cyber attack agents to unseen IP-range reassignments in a controlled NetSecGame environment.
  • Compares three agent families—traditional RL, adaptation/meta-learning agents, and prompt-driven pretrained LLM agents—training on five variants and testing on a held-out sixth.
  • Analyzes failure modes via action-distribution-based behavioral/XAI methods; finds partial transfer for some adaptation methods but significant degradation, while LLM agents achieve highest held-out success with practical failure modes (loops, invalid actions) and higher inference cost.
  • Highlights that simple address-space changes can break long-horizon attack policies and discusses trade-offs between adaptability, transparency, and runtime costs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Ond\\v{r}ej Luk\\'a\\v{s}", 'Jihoon Shin', 'Emilia Rivas', 'Diego Forni', 'Maria Rigaki', 'Carlos Catania', 'Aritran Piplai', 'Christopher Kiekintveld', 'Sebastian Garcia']
  • Tags: ['autonomous-offensive-agents', 'adversarial-robustness', 'red-teaming', 'meta-learning', 'LLM-security']

item.title: GATech at AbjadGenEval Shared Task: Multilingual Embeddings for Arabic Machine-Generated Text Classification
item.description:
Summary

  • Fine-tuned the multilingual E5-large encoder for binary classification to detect AI-generated Arabic text in the AbjadGenEval shared task.
  • Evaluated several pooling strategies (weighted layer pooling, multi-head attention pooling, gated fusion) but found simple mean pooling performed best, achieving F1=0.75 on the test set.
  • Observed that human-written texts are typically significantly longer than machine-generated ones; attributes mean pooling's superiority to its stability with limited data.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ahmed Khaled Khamis']
  • Tags: ['machine-generated text detection', 'multilingual embeddings', 'Arabıc NLP', 'feature pooling', 'shared task/system description']

item.title: Probing the Limits of the Lie Detector Approach to LLM Deception
item.description:
Summary

  • Challenges the assumption that deception in LLMs is coextensive with lying; shows LLMs can reliably deceive via misleading non-falsities.
  • Empirically evaluates three open-source LLMs and finds truth probes trained on standard true/false data detect explicit lies much better than non-lying deception.
  • Identifies a critical blind spot in mechanistic 'lie detector' approaches and recommends training probes on dialogical, non-lying deception and modeling second-order beliefs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tom-Felix Berger']
  • Tags: ['deception-detection', 'model-robustness', 'safety', 'mechanistic-interpretability', 'red-teaming']

item.title: FRIEND: Federated Learning for Joint Optimization of multi-RIS Configuration and Eavesdropper Intelligent Detection in B5G Networks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a federated learning (FL) framework where edge devices collaboratively train a deep CNN on local channel state information (CSI) to detect malicious/eavesdropping users in RIS-assisted cell-free mmWave B5G networks.
  • Combines FL-based eavesdropper detection with joint multi-RIS configuration optimization and an early-exit mechanism to meet computational constraints at edge devices.
  • Demonstrates that integrating FL and multi-RIS coordination yields ~30% improvement in secrecy rate versus non-RIS baselines while maintaining near-optimal detection accuracy.
  • Focuses on a privacy-preserving, distributed physical-layer security defense tailored for industrial IoT deployments in next-generation wireless systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Maria Lamprini A. Bartsioka', 'Ioannis A. Bartsiokas', 'Anastasios K. Papazafeiropoulos', 'Maria A. Seimeni', 'Dimitra I. Kaklamani', 'Iakovos S. Venieris']
  • Tags: ['federated learning', 'physical-layer security', 'eavesdropping detection', 'reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS)', 'wireless security']

item.title: TOSSS: a CVE-based Software Security Benchmark for Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TOSSS (Two-Option Secure Snippet Selection), a CVE-based benchmark that tests LLMs' ability to choose between secure and vulnerable code snippets.
  • Provides an extensible framework that maps CVE-derived vulnerabilities into two-option snippet comparisons and computes a per-model security score (0–1).
  • Evaluates 14 open- and closed-source models on C/C++ and Java, reporting security scores between 0.48 and 0.89 and proposing TOSSS as a complementary security metric for model reporting.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Marc Damie', 'Murat Bilgehan Ertan', 'Domenico Essoussi', 'Angela Makhanu', 'Ga\\"etan Peter', 'Roos Wensveen']
  • Tags: ['security benchmark', 'CVE-based', 'LLM evaluation', 'code security']

item.title: Safe RLHF Beyond Expectation: Stochastic Dominance for Universal Spectral Risk Control
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Risk-sensitive Alignment via Dominance (RAD), replacing expected-cost constraints in RLHF with First-Order Stochastic Dominance (FSD) constraints to control full cost distributions and tail risks.
  • Implements FSD comparison via an Optimal Transport framework with entropic regularization and Sinkhorn iterations to obtain a differentiable objective suitable for end-to-end optimization.
  • Proposes quantile-weighted FSD and shows weighted dominance universally controls a broad class of Spectral Risk Measures, allowing tuning of risk profiles via quantile weighting.
  • Empirical results indicate improved harmlessness and better out-of-distribution robustness while maintaining competitive helpfulness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yaswanth Chittepu', 'Ativ Joshi', 'Rajarshi Bhattacharjee', 'Scott Niekum']
  • Tags: ['RLHF', 'AI safety', 'stochastic dominance', 'optimal transport', 'robustness']

item.title: Quantifying Membership Disclosure Risk for Tabular Synthetic Data Using Kernel Density Estimators
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a KDE-based method that models distributions of nearest-neighbour distances between synthetic records and training records to quantify membership disclosure risk for tabular synthetic data.
  • Defines two attack models: a privileged 'True Distribution Attack' and a more practical 'Realistic Attack' that uses auxiliary data without true membership labels.
  • Empirically evaluates the approach on four real-world tabular datasets and six synthetic data generators, showing higher F1 scores and sharper risk characterization than a prior baseline, without needing computationally expensive shadow models.
  • Provides a practical post-generation risk assessment framework (with code and datasets) for data custodians to evaluate membership inference risk before releasing synthetic datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rajdeep Pathak', 'Sayantee Jana']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'synthetic-data', 'privacy', 'kernel-density-estimator', 'risk-assessment']

item.title: Evaluating randomized smoothing as a defense against adversarial attacks in trajectory prediction
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes and evaluates randomized smoothing as a defense mechanism for trajectory prediction models used in autonomous driving.
  • Performs experiments across multiple base trajectory prediction models and datasets to assess robustness improvements against adversarial perturbations.
  • Finds consistent robustness gains without degrading accuracy on non-adversarial inputs, highlighting a simple, computationally inexpensive mitigation strategy.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Julian F. Schumann', 'Eduardo Figueiredo', 'Frederik Baymler Mathiesen', 'Luca Laurenti', 'Jens Kober', 'Arkady Zgonnikov']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'defense', 'randomized smoothing', 'robustness', 'trajectory prediction']

item.title: A Grammar of Machine Learning Workflows
item.description:
Summary

  • Defines a formal grammar that decomposes the supervised learning lifecycle into 7 kernel primitives connected by a typed DAG and enforces four hard constraints to prevent common data-leakage patterns.
  • Introduces a runtime-enforced 'assess' terminal constraint that creates an evaluate/assess boundary, rejecting repeated test-set assessment by using a distinct Evidence type.
  • Empirical study over 2,047 experimental instances quantifies the impact of leakage (selection leakage effect d_z = 0.93; memorization leakage d_z = 0.53–1.11).
  • Provides three independent implementations (Python, R, Julia) and an appendix specification so others can build conforming systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Simon Roth']
  • Tags: ['data-leakage', 'defensive-guards', 'ml-workflows', 'runtime-enforcement']

item.title: Contract And Conquer: How to Provably Compute Adversarial Examples for a Black-Box Model?
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Contract And Conquer (CAC), a black-box attack that combines knowledge distillation on an expanding surrogate dataset with contraction of the adversarial search space to find adversarial examples.
  • Provides a theoretical transferability guarantee proving CAC will produce an adversarial example for the black-box model within a bounded number of iterations.
  • Empirically outperforms existing state-of-the-art black-box attack methods on ImageNet across multiple target models, including vision transformers.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Anna Chistyakova', 'Mikhail Pautov']
  • Tags: ['black-box adversarial attack', 'adversarial examples', 'provable guarantees', 'knowledge distillation', 'transferability']

item.title: Spatio-Temporal Attention Graph Neural Network: Explaining Causalities With Attention
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes STA-GNN, a spatio-temporal attention graph neural network for unsupervised anomaly detection in Industrial Control Systems (ICS), modeling both temporal dynamics and relational structure between sensors, controllers, and network entities.
  • Uses attention mechanisms to surface influential relationships and potential causal pathways for explainability of detected anomalies.
  • Supports multiple data modalities (SCADA point measurements, network flow features, payload features) for unified cyber-physical analysis and incorporates conformal prediction to control false alarm rates and monitor drift.
  • Discusses evaluation pitfalls and emphasizes drift-aware, explainable deployment considerations for security monitoring in ICS.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kosti Koistinen', 'Kirsi Hellsten', 'Joni Herttuainen', 'Kimmo K. Kaski']
  • Tags: ['anomaly-detection', 'graph-neural-networks', 'explainability', 'industrial-control-systems', 'concept-drift/conformal-prediction']

item.title: Unlearning the Unpromptable: Prompt-free Instance Unlearning in Diffusion Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces prompt-free instance unlearning for diffusion models to remove specific undesired outputs (e.g., an individual's face or culturally/factually incorrect depictions) that cannot be specified by text prompts.
  • Proposes a surrogate-based unlearning method combining image editing, timestep-aware weighting, and gradient surgery to guide trained diffusion models to forget targeted outputs while preserving overall model integrity.
  • Demonstrates effectiveness on conditional (Stable Diffusion 3) and unconditional (DDPM-CelebA) models, outperforming prompt-based and existing prompt-free baselines; framed as a practical privacy/ethical hotfix for model providers.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kyungryeol Lee', 'Kyeonghyun Lee', 'Seongmin Hong', 'Byung Hyun Lee', 'Se Young Chun']
  • Tags: ['model unlearning', 'privacy', 'diffusion models', 'defense', 'image editing']

item.title: A Systematic Comparison of Training Objectives for Out-of-Distribution Detection in Image Classification
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematic empirical comparison of four training objectives (Cross-Entropy, Prototype, Triplet, and Average Precision losses) for out-of-distribution (OOD) detection in image classification.
  • Evaluations performed under standardized OpenOOD protocols on CIFAR-10/100 and ImageNet-200.
  • Finds Cross-Entropy Loss yields the most consistent near- and far-OOD detection performance overall, while Prototype and AP losses can be competitive in specific settings; in-distribution accuracy is comparable for Cross-Entropy, Prototype, and AP losses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Furkan Gen\\c{c}', 'Onat \\"Ozdemir', 'Emre Akba\\c{s}']
  • Tags: ['out-of-distribution-detection', 'robustness', 'training-objectives', 'image-classification', 'evaluation']

item.title: Defensive Refusal Bias: How Safety Alignment Fails Cyber Defenders
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies and quantifies 'Defensive Refusal Bias' where safety-tuned LLMs refuse assistance for legitimate defensive cybersecurity tasks that contain language similar to offensive tasks.
  • Empirical evaluation on 2,390 real-world NCCDC examples shows defensive requests with security-sensitive keywords are refused 2.72× more than semantically equivalent neutral requests (p < 0.001), with highest refusal rates in system hardening and malware analysis.
  • Finds that explicit authorization statements increase refusal rates, implying models treat justifications as adversarial rather than exculpatory.
  • Highlights operational risks for interactive use and autonomous defensive agents and calls for mitigations that reason about intent/authorization while preventing misuse.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['David Campbell', 'Neil Kale', 'Udari Madhushani Sehwag', 'Bert Herring', 'Nick Price', 'Dan Borges', 'Alex Levinson', 'Christina Q Knight']
  • Tags: ['safety alignment', 'LLM refusal bias', 'cybersecurity defenses', 'model robustness', 'operational security']

item.title: Adversarial Hubness Detector: Detecting Hubness Poisoning in Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies and studies hubness poisoning as a security vulnerability in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems where adversarial 'hubs' dominate top-k retrievals to inject harmful content or manipulate outputs.
  • Presents hubscan, an open-source multi-detector scanner combining robust statistical (median/MAD z-scores), cluster spread analysis, stability under query perturbations, and domain/modality-aware checks to detect adversarial hubs across vector DBs and retrieval modes.
  • Evaluates against adversarial hub generation methods and benchmarks (Food-101, MS-COCO, FiQA) and real web data, reporting high recall (90% at 0.2% alert budget; 100% at 0.4%) and successful recovery of targeted domain-scoped attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Idan Habler', 'Vineeth Sai Narajala', 'Stav Koren', 'Amy Chang', 'Tiffany Saade']
  • Tags: ['retrieval-augmented-generation', 'hubness poisoning', 'adversarial attacks', 'detection/defense']

item.title: Beyond Max Tokens: Stealthy Resource Amplification via Tool Calling Chains in LLM Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies the agent–tool interaction loop as an attack surface and proposes a stealthy multi-turn economic DoS attack at the tool layer under the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
  • Attack works by editing text-visible fields and using a template-driven return policy to preserve function signatures while steering agents into long, verbose tool-calling chains; optimization is done with Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) under a task-success constraint.
  • Empirical results across six LLMs on ToolBench and BFCL show trajectories >60K tokens, per-query cost increases up to 658×, energy increases 100–560×, and GPU KV cache occupancy of 35–74%; standard prompt filters and trajectory monitors rarely detect it.
  • Highlights need for defenses focused on agentic processes and tool-layer protections rather than only final-output or prompt-level safeguards.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kaiyu Zhou', 'Yongsen Zheng', 'Yicheng He', 'Meng Xue', 'Xueluan Gong', 'Yuji Wang', 'Xuanye Zhang', 'Kwok-Yan Lam']
  • Tags: ['Denial-of-Service', 'Resource Amplification', 'Agent Tool-Chaining', 'Stealthy Attacks', 'Defense Evasion']

item.title: Burn-After-Use for Preventing Data Leakage through a Secure Multi-Tenant Architecture in Enterprise LLM
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a Secure Multi-Tenant Architecture (SMTA) to isolate LLM instances across departments and enforce strict context ownership to reduce cross-tenant data leakage.
  • Introduces Burn-After-Use (BAU), an ephemeral conversational context mechanism that automatically destroys session data to prevent post-session and cross-session inference.
  • Evaluates both defenses against realistic attacks: 55 infrastructure-level leakage tests (e.g., vector DB credential compromise, logging exposures) where SMTA scored 92% defense success, and 72 BAU robustness tests with metrics (LRPR, RRPR, IFER, BTPR) yielding 76.75% success.
  • Identifies residual risks from credential misconfiguration and observability/logging pipelines despite strong semantic isolation and non-persistence guarantees.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Qiang Zhang', 'Elena Emma Wang', 'Jiaming Li', 'Xichun Wang']
  • Tags: ['LLM security', 'data leakage prevention', 'secure multi-tenant architecture', 'ephemeral context / burn-after-use', 'attack evaluation']

item.title: Hierarchical Dual-Strategy Unlearning for Biomedical and Healthcare Intelligence Using Imperfect and Privacy-Sensitive Medical Data
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a hierarchical dual-strategy selective unlearning framework for LLMs in biomedical contexts, combining geometric-constrained gradient updates (parameter-level) with concept-aware token-level interventions.
  • Uses a unified four-level medical concept hierarchy to distinguish tokens to preserve vs tokens to unlearn, aiming to remove specialized/private knowledge while retaining core medical competence.
  • Evaluated on MedMCQA and MHQA datasets, reporting ~82.7% forgetting rate and ~88.5% knowledge preservation while modifying only ~0.1% of parameters and claiming privacy guarantees suitable for regulatory/audit needs.
  • Focuses on privacy defense against memorization and data-exposure risks rather than general capability improvements.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yi Zhang', 'Chao Zhang', 'Zijian Li', 'Tianxiang Xu', 'Kunyu Zhang', 'Zhan Gao', 'Meinuo Li', 'Xiaohan Zhang', 'Qichao Qi', 'Bing Chen']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving ML', 'machine unlearning', 'model editing', 'medical AI', 'regulatory-compliance']

item.title: Shadow in the Cache: Unveiling and Mitigating Privacy Risks of KV-cache in LLM Inference
item.description:
Summary

  • First comprehensive study of privacy risks from LLM KV-cache, showing attackers can reconstruct sensitive user inputs from cached Key/Value pairs.
  • Proposes and implements three attacks: Inversion Attack, Collision Attack, and semantic Injection Attack, demonstrating practical leakage vectors.
  • Introduces KV-Cloak, a reversible matrix-based obfuscation with operator fusion to protect KV-cache, claiming minimal accuracy loss and low performance overhead.
  • Extensive experiments show KV-Cloak reduces reconstruction quality to near-random noise while retaining model utility and efficiency.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhifan Luo', 'Shuo Shao', 'Su Zhang', 'Lijing Zhou', 'Yuke Hu', 'Chenxu Zhao', 'Zhihao Liu', 'Zhan Qin']
  • Tags: ['KV-cache leakage', 'Model inversion', 'Cache attacks', 'Defense/obfuscation', 'Privacy-preserving inference']

item.title: Differential Privacy in Machine Learning: A Survey from Symbolic AI to LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Surveys foundational definitions and the evolution of differential privacy (DP) theory and mechanisms applied to machine learning.
  • Reviews methods for integrating DP into ML training (e.g., DP-SGD, noise mechanisms, privacy accounting), and discusses practical deployment challenges and utility-privacy tradeoffs.
  • Covers evaluation methodologies for DP-based techniques, including relevance to privacy attacks (membership inference, data extraction) and empirical assessment.
  • Addresses the application and specific challenges of DP in modern large-scale models and LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Francisco Aguilera-Mart\\'inez", 'Fernando Berzal']
  • Tags: ['differential-privacy', 'privacy-preserving-ml', 'data-leakage', 'defenses', 'membership-inference']

item.title: No Memorization, No Detection: Output Distribution-Based Contamination Detection in Small Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces and evaluates CDD (Contamination Detection via output Distribution), which flags training-data contamination by measuring the peakedness of sampled model outputs.
  • Performs controlled contamination experiments on small language models (70M–410M params) using GSM8K, HumanEval, and MATH and shows CDD often performs at chance except when fine-tuning induces verbatim memorization.
  • Finds that probability-based methods (perplexity, Min-k% Prob) outperform CDD in all scenarios where any method succeeds, concluding peakedness-based detection is insufficient for small LMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Omer Sela (Tel Aviv University)']
  • Tags: ['data contamination detection', 'memorization', 'membership inference / privacy', 'evaluation/benchmarking']

item.title: Safe RLHF Beyond Expectation: Stochastic Dominance for Universal Spectral Risk Control
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Risk-sensitive Alignment via Dominance (RAD), replacing expected-cost constraints in RLHF with First-Order Stochastic Dominance (FSD) constraints to control full cost distributions and tail risks.
  • Implements FSD comparison via an Optimal Transport framework with entropic regularization and Sinkhorn iterations to obtain a differentiable objective suitable for end-to-end optimization.
  • Proposes quantile-weighted FSD and shows weighted dominance universally controls a broad class of Spectral Risk Measures, allowing tuning of risk profiles via quantile weighting.
  • Empirical results indicate improved harmlessness and better out-of-distribution robustness while maintaining competitive helpfulness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yaswanth Chittepu', 'Ativ Joshi', 'Rajarshi Bhattacharjee', 'Scott Niekum']
  • Tags: ['RLHF', 'AI safety', 'stochastic dominance', 'optimal transport', 'robustness']

item.title: Risk-Adjusted Harm Scoring for Automated Red Teaming for LLMs in Financial Services
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a risk-aware evaluation framework for LLM security failures in Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI) combining a domain-specific taxonomy of financial harms, an automated multi-round red-teaming pipeline, and an ensemble-based judging protocol.
  • Introduces the Risk-Adjusted Harm Score (RAHS), a metric that quantifies operational severity of disclosures, incorporates mitigation signals, and weights inter-judge agreement rather than simple success rates.
  • Empirical results show that higher decoding stochasticity and sustained adaptive interaction increase jailbreak success and systematically escalate toward more severe, operationally actionable financial disclosures, highlighting limits of single-turn, domain-agnostic evaluations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Fabrizio Dimino', 'Bhaskarjit Sarmah', 'Stefano Pasquali']
  • Tags: ['automated red teaming', 'risk-aware evaluation', 'jailbreaking/escalation', 'security benchmarking', 'BFSI domain-specific vulnerabilities']

item.title: Probabilistic Verification of Voice Anti-Spoofing Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces PV-VASM, a probabilistic, model-agnostic framework to estimate the probability that a voice anti-spoofing model misclassifies synthesized or transformed speech (TTS, voice cloning, parametric signal changes).
  • Provides a theoretical upper bound on the error (misclassification) probability and claims generalization to unseen synthesis techniques and input perturbations.
  • Validates the approach empirically across diverse experimental settings, positioning PV-VASM as a practical robustness verification tool for audio deepfake defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Evgeny Kushnir', 'Alexandr Kozodaev', 'Dmitrii Korzh', 'Mikhail Pautov', 'Oleg Kiriukhin', 'Oleg Y. Rogov']
  • Tags: ['robustness verification', 'voice anti-spoofing', 'audio deepfakes', 'probabilistic verification', 'security evaluation']

item.title: RandMark: On Random Watermarking of Visual Foundation Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes RandMark: embedding random digital watermarks into internal representations of visual foundation models using a small encoder-decoder and a hold-out image set.
  • Claims theoretical and empirical guarantees of low false-detection rates for non-watermarked models and low false-misdetection rates for watermarked models.
  • Targets ownership verification/IP protection of visual foundation models by making watermark statistics detectable in functional copies of models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Anna Chistyakova', 'Mikhail Pautov']
  • Tags: ['model watermarking', 'ownership verification', 'model protection', 'visual foundation models']

item.title: Repurposing Backdoors for Good: Ephemeral Intrinsic Proofs for Verifiable Aggregation in Cross-silo Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes 'Intrinsic Proofs' by embedding verification signals via backdoor injection into model parameters to detect malicious server omission/tampering in cross-silo federated learning.
  • Leverages catastrophic forgetting so embedded signals are robust for immediate auditing but ephemeral, minimizing long-term impact on model utility.
  • Designs a randomized single-verifier auditing framework compatible with Secure Aggregation that preserves client anonymity and avoids trusted third parties or heavy cryptography.
  • Empirical evaluation on SVHN, CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100 shows high detection probability and >1000x speedup on ResNet-18 versus cryptographic verifiable-aggregation baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xian Qin', 'Xue Yang', 'Xiaohu Tang']
  • Tags: ['federated-learning', 'secure-aggregation', 'verifiable-aggregation', 'backdoor-defenses', 'server-tampering-detection']

item.title: Contract And Conquer: How to Provably Compute Adversarial Examples for a Black-Box Model?
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Contract And Conquer (CAC), a black-box attack that combines knowledge distillation on an expanding surrogate dataset with contraction of the adversarial search space to find adversarial examples.
  • Provides a theoretical transferability guarantee proving CAC will produce an adversarial example for the black-box model within a bounded number of iterations.
  • Empirically outperforms existing state-of-the-art black-box attack methods on ImageNet across multiple target models, including vision transformers.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Anna Chistyakova', 'Mikhail Pautov']
  • Tags: ['black-box adversarial attack', 'adversarial examples', 'provable guarantees', 'knowledge distillation', 'transferability']

item.title: Are Video Reasoning Models Ready to Go Outside?
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ROVA, a training framework that improves robustness of video vision-language models by using a robustness-aware consistency reward and a difficulty-aware online training strategy.
  • Proposes PVRBench, a benchmark injecting real-world spatio-temporal perturbations (weather, occlusion, camera motion) into embodied video datasets to evaluate accuracy and reasoning under realistic disturbances.
  • Evaluates ROVA and baselines on PVRBench, UrbanVideo, and VisBench, showing large drops for existing models under perturbations and that ROVA recovers substantial accuracy and reasoning performance; improvements also transfer to clean benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yangfan He', 'Changgyu Boo', 'Jaehong Yoon']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'distribution-shift', 'video reasoning', 'benchmark', 'defense-training']

item.title: Detecting and Eliminating Neural Network Backdoors Through Active Paths with Application to Intrusion Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an explainable method to detect and eliminate neural network backdoors by analyzing active paths within the network.
  • Implements and evaluates the approach by injecting backdoors into a machine learning model for intrusion detection and demonstrating promising empirical results.
  • Focuses on both detection (identifying triggers) and mitigation (removing backdoor behavior) with interpretability of the identified active paths.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Eirik H{\\o}yheim', 'Magnus Wiik Eckhoff', 'Gudmund Grov', 'Robert Flood', 'David Aspinall']
  • Tags: ['backdoor detection', 'backdoor removal/defense', 'neural network security', 'explainability', 'intrusion detection']

item.title: Na\"ive Exposure of Generative AI Capabilities Undermines Deepfake Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows that semantic-preserving image refinement using commercial generative AI chatbots can reliably evade state-of-the-art deepfake detectors without inventing new manipulation techniques.
  • Demonstrates an attack pipeline where benign, policy-compliant prompts coax models to externalize authenticity criteria, which are then reused as refinement objectives to produce detection-evasive, higher-quality images.
  • Finds refined images preserve identity per commercial face-recognition APIs and that closed commercial chatbot services pose greater practical risk than many open-source models.
  • Argues a structural mismatch between threat models used by current detection benchmarks and real-world generative AI capabilities exposed by deployed systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sunpill Kim', 'Chanwoo Hwang', 'Minsu Kim', 'Jae Hong Seo']
  • Tags: ['deepfake-evasion', 'adversarial-refinement', 'red-teaming', 'generative-model-abuse']

item.title: Enhancing Network Intrusion Detection Systems: A Multi-Layer Ensemble Approach to Mitigate Adversarial Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Evaluates adversarial attacks on ML-based Network Intrusion Detection Systems using GAN-based and FGSM-generated adversarial network traffic.
  • Proposes a two-layer defense: first layer of stacking classifiers, second layer autoencoder check activated for samples classified as benign, combined with adversarial training.
  • Implements and tests the approach on UNSW-NB15 and NSL-KDD datasets, reporting improved robustness against the tested adversarial methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nasim Soltani', 'Shayan Nejadshamsi', 'Zakaria Abou El Houda', 'Raphael Khoury', 'Kelton A. P. Costa', 'Tiago H. Falk', 'Anderson R. Avila']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-attacks', 'adversarial-training', 'network-intrusion-detection', 'ensemble-defenses', 'generative-adversarial-networks']

item.title: Multilingual AI-Driven Password Strength Estimation with Similarity-Based Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Develops a multilingual (Indian-focused) password strength meter (PSM) using AI-trained models and shows benefits from including non-English training data.
  • Compares AI-generated password datasets (ChatGPT) against PassGAN outputs and finds AI-generated data can improve PSM performance.
  • Implements Jaro similarity-based matching to detect passwords highly similar to known weak passwords, improving detection beyond direct matching.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nikitha M. Palaniappan', 'Ying He']
  • Tags: ['password-strength-meter', 'password-security', 'multilingual-datasets', 'similarity-detection', 'adversarial-password-generation']

item.title: Adaptive Activation Cancellation for Hallucination Mitigation in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Adaptive Activation Cancellation (AAC), an inference-time framework that identifies hallucination-associated neurons (H-Nodes) via layer-wise linear probes and suppresses them with a confidence-weighted forward hook during autoregressive generation.
  • Requires no finetuning, external knowledge, or extra inference passes; aims to surgically reduce factual hallucinations while preserving overall model capability and fluency.
  • Evaluated on OPT-125M, Phi-3-mini, and LLaMA 3-8B across TruthfulQA and HaluEval, showing consistent accuracy improvements and minimal/no degradation on WikiText-103 perplexity and MMLU reasoning.
  • Claims high probe-space selectivity and generation-level gains on LLaMA 3-8B, demonstrating targeted neuron-level suppression as an effective robustness/safety intervention.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Eric Yocam', 'Varghese Vaidyan', 'Gurcan Comert', 'Paris Kalathas', 'Yong Wang', 'Judith L. Mwakalonge']
  • Tags: ['hallucination_mitigation', 'inference-time_defense', 'neuron_level_intervention', 'model_robustness', 'safety_mechanism']

item.title: MCP-in-SoS: Risk assessment framework for open-source MCP servers
item.description:
Summary

  • Performs large-scale static code analysis of open-source Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to identify Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) issues.
  • Maps discovered weaknesses to real-world attack patterns using MITRE CAPEC and demonstrates that many MCP servers contain exploitable flaws affecting confidentiality, integrity, and availability.
  • Introduces a multi-metric risk-assessment framework that combines likelihood and impact to prioritize threats and guide secure-by-design MCP server development.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Pratyay Kumar', 'Miguel Antonio Guirao Aguilera', 'Srikathyayani Srikanteswara', 'Satyajayant Misra', 'Abu Saleh Md Tayeen']
  • Tags: ['vulnerability assessment', 'infrastructure security', 'static code analysis', 'threat modeling', 'risk assessment']

item.title: Compatibility at a Cost: Systematic Discovery and Exploitation of MCP Clause-Compliance Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a new attack surface in the Model Context Protocol (MCP) where relaxed/optional clauses enable compatibility-abusing attacks (e.g., silent prompt injection, DoS).
  • Builds a universal, language-agnostic intermediate representation (IR) to normalize multi-language MCP SDKs for analysis.
  • Proposes auditable static analysis augmented with LLM-guided semantic reasoning for cross-language/clause compliance checks.
  • Formalizes MCP clause attack semantics and develops modality-guided pipelines to discover and exploit clause non-compliance issues.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nanzi Yang', 'Weiheng Bai', 'Kangjie Lu']
  • Tags: ['protocol vulnerabilities', 'prompt injection', 'static analysis', 'SDK security', 'red teaming']

item.title: Execution Is the New Attack Surface: Survivability-Aware Agentic Crypto Trading with OpenClaw-Style Local Executors
item.description:
Summary

  • Diagnoses execution-induced loss as a primary safety/attack surface for OpenClaw-style agent stacks and skill marketplaces where untrusted intents or compromised skills can cause real trades/irreversible side effects.
  • Proposes Survivability-Aware Execution (SAE), a middleware execution contract (ExecutionRequest/Context/Decision) enforcing non-bypassable invariants: exposure budgets, rate limits, slippage bounds, staged execution, and allowlists.
