  • Operationalizes the Delegation Gap via a logged Intended Policy Spec enabling out-of-scope labeling and reproducible DG metrics; evaluates SAE on offline Binance perpetual replay showing large survivability improvements and statistical significance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ailiya Borjigin', 'Igor Stadnyk', 'Ben Bilski', 'Serhii Hovorov', 'Sofiia Pidturkina']
  • Tags: ['execution-layer defenses', 'agentic systems security', 'supply-chain risk / skills security', 'financial trading safety', 'attack surface reduction']

item.title: Multi-Stream Perturbation Attack: Breaking Safety Alignment of Thinking LLMs Through Concurrent Task Interference
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces the multi-stream perturbation attack that interweaves multiple task streams in a single prompt to disrupt LLM thinking-mode reasoning and bypass safety alignment.
  • Presents three perturbation strategies: multi-stream interleaving, inversion perturbation (character reversal), and shape transformation (format constraints) to induce interference and collapse of reasoning.
  • Evaluates the method on JailbreakBench, AdvBench, and HarmBench across multiple commercial/open models (Qwen3 series, DeepSeek, Qwen3-Max, Gemini 2.5 Flash), reporting high attack success rates and increased thinking collapse and repetition rates.
  • Demonstrates a practical safety bypass and destabilization vector for LLMs, highlighting risks in multi-task/interleaved prompt scenarios.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Fan Yang']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'prompt injection', 'adversarial perturbation', 'safety bypass', 'red teaming']

item.title: Amnesia: Adversarial Semantic Layer Specific Activation Steering in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Amnesia, an activation-space adversarial attack that manipulates transformer internal states to bypass safety mechanisms in open-weight LLMs.
  • Demonstrates the attack on state-of-the-art open-weight models and shows it can induce harmful/antisocial behaviors without fine-tuning or additional training.
  • Includes red-teaming experiments and benchmark evaluations illustrating the attack's effectiveness against existing safeguards, highlighting a security vulnerability in LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ali Raza', 'Gurang Gupta', 'Nikolay Matyunin', 'Jibesh Patra']
  • Tags: ['activation-space attack', 'adversarial attack', 'LLM safety', 'red-teaming', 'model internals']

item.title: TASER: Task-Aware Spectral Energy Refine for Backdoor Suppression in UAV Swarms Decentralized Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies that stealthy backdoor gradients in UAV decentralized federated learning exhibit distinctive spectral concentration when attackers mimic benign behaviors.
  • Proposes TASER (Task-Aware Spectral Energy Refine), a decentralized defense that preserves main-task-relevant frequency coefficients and discards others to suppress backdoor tasks without relying on outlier detection.
  • Provides theoretical guarantees and empirical results showing TASER reduces attack success rate below 20% while keeping accuracy loss under 5% in resource-constrained UAV-DFL settings.
  • Targets practicality for UAV swarms by avoiding complex global coordination and heavy outlier-detection mechanisms, using efficient gradient spectral analysis instead.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sizhe Huang', 'Shujie Yang']
  • Tags: ['backdoor defense', 'decentralized federated learning', 'spectral analysis', 'UAV/swarm security', 'gradient-based defenses']

item.title: Why LLMs Fail: A Failure Analysis and Partial Success Measurement for Automated Security Patch Generation
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes 319 LLM-generated security patches for 64 Java vulnerabilities from the Vul4J benchmark using tri-axis evaluation (compilation, security via PoV tests, functionality via test suites).
  • Finds only 24.8% of patches fully correct while 51.4% fail both security and functionality; main failure mode is semantic misunderstanding (syntactically valid but incorrect repair strategy).
  • Proposes the Security Repair Score (SRS) to quantify preservation of functionality versus security improvement (mean functionality 0.832, mean security 0.251).
  • Shows fixability varies by vulnerability type (e.g., 0% for input validation, 45% for infinite loop) and emphasizes the need for rigorous validation before deploying LLM-generated patches.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Amir Al-Maamari']
  • Tags: ['automated program repair', 'security patches', 'LLMs', 'vulnerability analysis', 'benchmarking']

item.title: ADVERSA: Measuring Multi-Turn Guardrail Degradation and Judge Reliability in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents ADVERSA, an automated red-teaming framework that measures multi-turn guardrail degradation as continuous per-round compliance trajectories instead of binary pass/fail jailbreak outcomes.
  • Uses a fine-tuned 70B attacker model (ADVERSA-Red) to remove attacker-side refusals and scores victim replies on a structured 5-point rubric, treating partial compliance as a measurable state.
  • Runs controlled experiments on three frontier LLMs (Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, GPT-5.2) with up to 10 adversarial rounds and a triple-judge consensus architecture that explicitly measures judge reliability.
  • Finds a 26.7% jailbreak rate with average jailbreak round 1.25 (early-round concentration), documents inter-judge agreement rates, attacker drift, attacker refusals as a confound, and releases artifacts while withholding attack prompts.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Harry Owiredu-Ashley']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-evaluation', 'jailbreaking', 'red-teaming', 'guardrail-degradation', 'judge-reliability']

item.title: Tool Receipts, Not Zero-Knowledge Proofs: Practical Hallucination Detection for AI Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes NabaOS, a lightweight runtime verification framework that labels LLM claims by epistemic source and uses HMAC-signed tool execution receipts to prevent/detect fabricated tool outputs and other hallucinations in agent responses.
  • Introduces NyayaVerifyBench, a 1,800-scenario multilingual benchmark with injected hallucination types, and reports high detection rates (e.g., 94.2% fabricated tool references, 87.6% count misstatements) with <15ms verification overhead.
  • Compares receipt-based verification against cryptographic proofs (zkLLM), sampling, hashing, tensor commitments, and self-consistency, arguing receipts offer the best cost-latency-coverage trade-off for interactive agents.
  • Demonstrates handling of deep delegation (multi-step web tasks) with independent re-fetch checks catching 78.4% of URL fabrications.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Abhinaba Basu']
  • Tags: ['hallucination detection', 'tool-use verification', 'runtime defenses', 'signed receipts (HMAC)', 'security benchmark']

item.title: Targeted Bit-Flip Attacks on LLM-Based Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Flip-Agent, a framework for targeted bit-flip attacks (BFAs) against LLM-based agents that manipulates model parameters to change final outputs and tool invocations.
  • Extends prior BFA work (focused on single-step models) to multi-stage agent pipelines and external tool use, identifying new attack surfaces.
  • Empirically outperforms existing targeted BFAs on real-world agent tasks, demonstrating a critical vulnerability in agent systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jialai Wang', 'Ya Wen', 'Zhongmou Liu', 'Yuxiao Wu', 'Bingyi He', 'Zongpeng Li', 'Ee-Chien Chang']
  • Tags: ['bit-flip attack', 'fault injection', 'LLM agents', 'integrity/poisoning', 'adversarial attack']

item.title: Measuring and Eliminating Refusals in Military Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a gold-standard benchmark (created by military veterans) to measure refusal and deflection rates of LLMs on military-related queries.
  • Evaluates refusal/deflection rates across 31 public and 3 military models, finding hard rejection rates up to 98.2% and soft deflections up to 21.3%.
  • Demonstrates use of the Heretic library to ablate safety behaviors on a military-tuned model, increasing answer rate by 66.5 percentage points while modestly affecting other task accuracy; argues for specialized training to achieve zero refusals in closed military models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jack FitzGerald', 'Dylan Bates', 'Aristotelis Lazaridis', 'Aman Sharma', 'Vincent Lu', 'Brian King', 'Yousif Azami', 'Sean Bailey', 'Jeremy Cao', 'Peter Damianov', 'Kevin de Haan', 'Joseph Madigan', 'Jeremy McLaurin', 'Luke Kerbs', 'Jonathan Tainer', 'Dave Anderson', 'Jonathan Beck', 'Jamie Cuticello', 'Colton Malkerson', 'Tyler Saltsman']
  • Tags: ['refusal/jailbreak', 'safety bypass', 'red teaming', 'benchmarking']

item.title: FERRET: Framework for Expansion Reliant Red Teaming
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces FERRET, an automated red teaming framework to generate multi-modal adversarial conversations aimed at breaking target models.
  • Defines three expansion strategies: horizontal (self-improving conversation starters), vertical (expanding starters into full multi-modal conversations), and meta (discovering more effective multi-modal attack strategies during a conversation).
  • Compares FERRET to existing automated red teaming approaches and reports superior effectiveness in producing multi-modal adversarial conversations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ninareh Mehrabi', 'Vitor Albiero', 'Maya Pavlova', 'Joanna Bitton']
  • Tags: ['red-teaming', 'adversarial-attacks', 'automated-red-team', 'multimodal-attacks', 'security-framework']

item.title: A Retrieval-Augmented Language Assistant for Unmanned Aircraft Safety Assessment and Regulatory Compliance
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a retrieval-augmented assistant that grounds responses in authoritative regulatory sources to support unmanned aircraft safety assessment and regulatory compliance.
  • Implements system-level controls—separating evidence storage from language generation, enforcing citation-driven outputs, and adopting conservative behaviors—to mitigate generative model failure modes (fabrications, unsupported inferences, unclear provenance).
  • Evaluates retrieval strategies, interaction constraints, and response policies for suitability in safety-sensitive regulatory workflows and provides guidance for integration while preserving human oversight and accountability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Gabriele Immordino', 'Andrea Vaiuso', 'Marcello Righi']
  • Tags: ['retrieval-augmented-generation', 'hallucination-mitigation', 'provenance and traceability', 'safety guardrails', 'regulatory compliance']

item.title: Explainable LLM Unlearning Through Reasoning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes reasoning-based unlearning targets and a method (TRU) to perform targeted LLM unlearning that combines supervised cross-entropy loss with gradient-ascent-based loss.
  • Aims to precisely remove specified knowledge while preserving unrelated model capabilities and producing coherent post-unlearning responses.
  • Evaluates TRU across benchmarks and LLM backbones, reporting improved reliability and robustness under diverse attack scenarios compared to baselines.
  • Positions reasoning-augmented unlearning as an explainable, practical defense for safety, copyright, and privacy concerns in pre-trained LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research Paper

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Junfeng Liao', 'Qizhou Wang', 'Shanshan Ye', 'Xin Yu', 'Ling Chen', 'Zhen Fang']
  • Tags: ['model unlearning', 'privacy', 'defense', 'LLM safety']

item.title: IH-Challenge: A Training Dataset to Improve Instruction Hierarchy on Frontier LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces IH-Challenge, a reinforcement-learning fine-tuning dataset to improve instruction hierarchy (IH) handling in LLMs—i.e., prioritizing system/developer/user/tool instructions to resolve conflicts.
  • Targets defenses against jailbreaks, system prompt extraction, and agentic prompt injection via adversarial example generation and online fine-tuning.
  • Reports empirical gains: +10.0% IH robustness across 16 benchmarks, substantial reduction in unsafe behaviors (6.6% -> 0.7%), improved helpfulness, and minimal capability regression.
  • Releases the IH-Challenge dataset publicly to support future research on robust instruction hierarchy and adversarial training for LLM safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chuan Guo (Michael Pokorny)', 'Juan Felipe Ceron Uribe (Michael Pokorny)', 'Sicheng Zhu (Michael Pokorny)', 'Christopher A. Choquette-Choo (Michael Pokorny)', 'Steph Lin (Michael Pokorny)', 'Nikhil Kandpal (Michael Pokorny)', 'Milad Nasr (Michael Pokorny)', 'Rai (Michael Pokorny)', 'Sam Toyer', 'Miles Wang', 'Yaodong Yu', 'Alex Beutel', 'Kai Xiao']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'jailbreak mitigation', 'prompt injection', 'adversarial training', 'red teaming/benchmark']
